import pandas as pd
import regex as re
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
COLUMN_NAMES = ["Name", "Email", "Age", "Country"]

def task_func(text):
    # Extract data using the given regular expression pattern
    pattern = r'Name: (.*?), Email: (.*?), Age: (.*?), Country: (.*?)($|\n)'
    matches = re.findall(pattern, text, re.DOTALL)

    # Create a Pandas DataFrame from the extracted data
    df = pd.DataFrame(matches, columns=COLUMN_NAMES)

    # Clean and format the data if needed (you can add more clean-up operations based on your specific requirements)
    df['Age'] = df['Age'].astype(int)

    # Plot age distribution using seaborn
    plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
    sns.histplot(df['Age'], bins=range(min(df['Age']), max(df['Age'])+1))
    plt.xlabel('Age')
    plt.ylabel('Count')
    plt.title('Age Distribution')

    return df, plt.gcf()
